best.name: Blockchain.info BTC (rawaddr)
best.notes: Wired in src/tools/crypto_tools.py::BlockchainBtcTool. Returns final_balance, n_tx, total_received/sent, and per-tx input/output addresses (connected wallets). Aggressive rate-limiting on the free host; for production add Blockstream/mempool as fallbacks below.

best.name: Etherscan (free tier, V2)
best.maintained: active; V2 multichain (60+ EVM chains via one key, 5 calls/sec free)
best.notes: Wired as EtherscanTool in crypto_tools.py but uses the LEGACY v1 URL (api.etherscan.io/api) with NO apikey. V1 was sunset May 31 2025 and the free tier now requires a key. ACTION: migrate to V2 endpoint above + add a free API key. Balance via action=balance; tokentx for ERC-20 transfers. chainid swaps to BSC/Arbitrum/Base/etc for cross-chain (capability below).

best.name: Blockstream Esplora (BTC)
best.maintained: active; Blockstream public infra since 2021, no key
best.notes: No-key BTC explorer. /address returns chain_stats + mempool_stats {tx_count, funded_txo_sum, spent_txo_sum} -> balance = funded - spent (sats). /txs returns last 50 mempool + 25 confirmed, /txs/chain/:last_seen_txid pages 25 at a time. Best free no-key fallback when blockchain.info rate-limits. Self-hostable. Same API works for Liquid.

runner_up.name: mempool.space REST API
runner_up.notes: Esplora-compatible schema (same fields as Blockstream), no key. Good second BTC fallback; also exposes fee/UTXO data. Enterprise tier exists but public API is free.

runner_up.name: Blockchair address dashboards (multi-chain)
runner_up.notes: One schema across 17+ chains incl. BTC and ETH -> single wrapper covers many selector types. Free tier 30 req/min, no key; some queries cost >1 'request point'. Strong unifier but free limits are tight; key it for volume.

capabilities.gaps: All free explorers cap returned tx pages (10-50); deep history (1000s of txs) needs paging loops or a keyed plan. Token/ERC-20 balances require a separate Etherscan call (tokentx/tokenbalance). No free tool gives USD-valued balance history without a price feed.

best.name: Connected-wallet extraction (input/output co-spend)
best.notes: BlockchainBtcTool already emits all input/output addresses per tx as crypto_btc entities. The classic common-input-ownership (co-spend) clustering heuristic can be applied by the supervisor over these inputs. This is the only FREE programmatic clustering we can do without a paid analytics vendor.

best.name: GraphSense (Iknaio hosted REST + graphsense-python)
best.notes: The ONLY open-source-grade address-clustering engine with a real API. Hosted instance (api.ikna.io) returns entity/cluster membership, address tags, and neighbor flows for BTC/ETH/TRON. Key is FREE-on-request (email contact@graphsense.org) but gated -> not zero-friction; mark free=false until a key is held. Self-hostable (Cassandra/Spark) for full sovereignty. Best clustering pick once keyed.

runner_up.name: GraphSense self-hosted (graphsense-lib CLI)
runner_up.notes: Full clustering with data sovereignty but heavy infra (Cassandra + Spark + full-node ingest). Not realistic for a lightweight gatherer; note as the sovereign fallback.

capabilities.gaps: Production-grade multi-heuristic clustering (peeling chains, change-address detection, cross-tx entity merge) is paid (Chainalysis/TRM/Elliptic) or self-hosted GraphSense. Free path is limited to co-spend heuristic on explorer output + keyed GraphSense lookups.

best.name: OFAC sanctioned digital-currency addresses (0xB10C)
best.maintained: active (auto-generated from Treasury SDN advanced XML; covers 17 assets)
best.notes: Definitive, authoritative sanctions attribution. Local set-membership check -> if hit, label = sanctioned entity (high-confidence 'confirmed'). Cache the list, refresh daily. ultrasoundmoney/ofac-ethereum-addresses is an ETH-only mirror. Zero-friction, no key, no rate limit.

best.name: Etherscan address labels (brianleect/etherscan-labels JSON/CSV dump)
best.install: none (download combined dump, load into dict)
best.maintained: periodic refresh; community label dumps for top EVM chains
best.notes: Maps ETH addresses -> exchange/service/protocol labels (e.g. 'Coinbase', 'Binance', 'Tornado Cash'). Etherscan's own nametag API endpoint exists but is PRO PLUS only (paid). This static dump is the free substitute: local lookup, no key. Pair with eth-labels.com public API (swagger at https://eth-labels.com/swagger) for fresher lookups (170k+ labels) when online.

runner_up.name: Arkham Intelligence Intel API
runner_up.notes: Best-in-class probabilistic attribution (entity labels across chains). Freemium dashboard but the Intel API requires requested access / paid tier; attribution is probabilistic not binary. Use as the keyed last-mile attribution upgrade, not a free default. Docs: api-guide.intel.arkm.com.

runner_up.name: Walletlabels (7.5M+ ETH labels)
runner_up.run: query interface over 7.5M+ labeled ETH addresses
runner_up.notes: Large ETH label corpus; web search free, programmatic API is paid. Runner-up to the free static dumps above.

capabilities.gaps: Free attribution = static lists only (OFAC sanctions + community label dumps), which lag real-time and miss un-labeled/fresh service wallets. Live, probabilistic, cross-chain entity attribution (Arkham/Chainalysis/Nansen/Breadcrumbs) is all keyed/paid. No free CLI/HTTP gives confident exchange deposit-address attribution at scale.

best.name: Blockchair unified multi-chain dashboards
best.run: GET https://api.blockchair.com/{chain}/dashboards/address/{selector}  (one schema for BTC, ETH, LTC, DOGE, BCH, DASH, ZEC, BSV, Groestlcoin + testnets)
best.notes: Single normalized response across UTXO and account chains -> one wrapper services many selector types with the same parser. Free 30 req/min no key. Best free unifier for breadth-of-chains coverage.

best.name: Etherscan V2 multichain (chainid switch)
best.maintained: active (60+ EVM chains, one free key, 5 req/sec shared; ~90% of chains in free tier)
best.notes: Cross-EVM-chain coverage from the SAME key/code by varying chainid -> covers the entire EVM side (an address appears on many EVM chains). Pairs with the existing EtherscanTool migration. Does NOT cover non-EVM (BTC, Solana, TRON) -> use Blockchair for UTXO chains.

runner_up.name: 3xpl.com universal explorer API
runner_up.notes: Fast ad-free universal explorer spanning many UTXO + account chains under one API; free key. Good Blockchair alternative for cross-chain breadth.

runner_up.name: GraphSense cross-ledger links
runner_up.notes: Iknaio GraphSense links entities across BTC/ETH/TRON; keyed. The analytics-grade cross-chain option once GraphSense is wired (see clustering).

capabilities.gaps: True cross-chain ENTITY linkage (same actor across BTC<->ETH<->Solana, bridge tracing) is paid/keyed (Arkham, GraphSense, Breadcrumbs). Free tools give parallel per-chain lookups (query each chain for the address) but not automated bridge/swap correlation. Non-EVM + non-UTXO chains (Solana, TON, Cosmos) need their own explorers (Solscan, TonAPI).

excluded.reason: Etherscan website (manual/web_interface) duplicate of the wired Etherscan API tool
blockchain.com/explorer web UI; covered by the wired blockchain.info rawaddr API
walletexplorer.com is a browser-only clustering site, no API; manual-only
Blockpath web interface, no programmatic API
Breadcrumbs.app — strong clustering/visualization but web UI + paid API; no free automatable endpoint
Walletlabels API is paid; free access is web-only (kept as paid runner-up under attribution)
OXT.me (Samourai) BTC clustering explorer effectively offline/seized; do not wire
alerting/monitoring service, paid, not an OSINT lookup
bitcoinabuse.com merged into Chainabuse (Chainalysis); free API deprecated/gated — re-evaluate Chainabuse API separately if needed
Enterprise attribution/clustering (Chainalysis, TRM, Elliptic) — no free tier, license-only
Nansen wallet labels — paid subscription, no free automatable API
Etherscan native nametags/metadata endpoint is PRO PLUS (paid) tier only; use free static label dumps instead
